item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could easily see this thing being keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the inability to contain it

You answered yourself here. Object Class isn't about danger, it's about difficulty of containment. It's an incredibly hard thing to contain, so it is assigned a higher object class. No matter how benign it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Classification upgrade to Keter is under consideration.

Why though?
Aside from being "spooky" and exposing the public to anomalies, there is no inherent danger here. The only thing keeping it from being safe is the inability to contain it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This skip would ruin any and all porn videos if it got out of YouTube. Or…enhance them if you're a sick fuck. +1 just for that.

item.title: I know this is late but...
item.content:encoded: Stay safe everybody and a big fat F for Stefan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Apparently, the guy in the interview is supposed to be Youtube personality JonTron… but how was I supposed to know that?! I don't watch JonTron, I don't know his full name or birthdate or catchphrases or whatever. What- I don't know- how-
WAHUH?!?!?!

item.title: RIP Karl Stefansson
item.content:encoded: Not sure if necroposting, but may he rest in peace.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He may be 3006, but he will always be 001 in our hearts

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: RIP Stefán Karl you will be always number one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This whole article is a meme, and I love it. It manages to be both funny and horrifying at the same time. Also, this skip is a bit sad now considering Stefan Karl Stefansson has died of cancer. May he remain, forever and always, number 1, and be immortalized forever in this skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the usage of the phrase "intense viscera pressure". But I don't really understand why all the duplicated people want to kill each other, especially if it's only after a small number of duplications.

item.title: Re: Grep Gorps
item.content:encoded:
Found this in the description:

If the subject views a series of videos the effect will also carry over to the next one.

That seems like it covers your misunderstanding.

item.title: Grep Gorps
item.content:encoded: Hey, I may be totally misunderstanding things here, but if the replicating effect is based on playback, then why does the Grumps section have sequential videos? If I'm to understand playback as "the reproduction of previously recorded audio and/or video recordings", then the experiment's watching a series of Grump videos would do nothing. The researchers would have to replay episode one repeatedly, yes? correct me if I'm at all off-base here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Essentially,"We are are number one but it's an SCP.". Absolutely genius. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I joined this whole site just so I can comment this - the best and scariest part about this article is actually not the SCP, but the original Colin Furze video, which when you think of it, actually was a video of a murder - the single victim being blown up with the van, and posted on the internet where nobody would know… Only thanks to the SCP are we informed on there being a human inside the vehicle. Brilliant and bone-chilling.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that due to the frequent vandalism attempts on this page, it is now locked. Those who have suggestions for fixes may comment them in the discussion thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A memetic meme…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting that a recent edit adding improper and inappropriate tags was reverted. Editors, please let our tag team handle adding tags.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
DarkPokemon, what you did was essentially vandalism, which is against the rules and tends to be punishable by a ban.

Since it looks like you were attempting to joke around, I won't be enacting a ban for this infraction, but this is a warning that any further such behavior will result in disciplinary action.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Played with the tag, it's fun :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This references a hilarious internet meme but accomplishes disturbing horror… How! Although I loved this I couldn't exactly see this becoming a 3000 article ):. Better look next time. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks man!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps it's because I read this long after the 3000 contest, but my impression of this article is much more positive than many of the opinions expressed here. The memes are but a means to an end, and the straight-faced gruesome imagery compliments them excellently. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this is too memey to not be a joke SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have thought about it and after a couple of rereads, I've decided that I really like this.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Adding to that, not only does this anomaly not have to actually be viewed (but simply posted) and, in the end, it states that it's even been discovered in Foundation video archives, meaning it's definitely not close to contained.

If I'm not misunderstanding this, I agree fully that it should be designated Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I go by difficulty in enacting containment procedures to determine object class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a bit confused, why would this be upgraded to Keter? While highly disturbing and difficult to contain, it doesn't seem to actually be dangerous, so why would they upgrade it to keter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A -1 from me, because I feel that if you take out the "we are number one except-" viral internet sensation reference, what remains isn't particularly strong or unique, and to me it feels like it's just exploiting the popularity of that meme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nah. This didn't do it for me. As a person who has the actual fear of claustrophobia and has had nightmares similar to this in the past, this just reads like you were trying to cram in internet celebrities and memes (not like the ones here) into an article just to say you did. Judging from the past comments, I also kind of get that that's what happened.

There's nothing wrong with this normally in an article, in fact, I think some of the better articles come out from these challenges that one user usually gives to another. But this one just didn't do it for me, I had to downvote. Whether it was because it directly used, "We Are Number One" as the source, or the Game Grumps were in it, but it just doesn't…yeah.

As a final note, similar to what everyone else said, the interview logs and previous addendums were actually really well written. The last interview actually gave me a little freak-out. Would the effects not be prevalent if the interview just wasn't recorded on video?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is Mr. Blackbox (interveiwee) "Dan Avidan" or "Jon Jafari", sounds like Jon but I can't tell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll agree that this piece is a bit similar to 1733, but it's still good enough to get a +1 from me.

item.title: Re: Security
item.content:encoded: Enclosed spaces become sealed.

item.title: Security
item.content:encoded: Wouldn't the security fly into the room or already be in there like they're supposed to be and start pouring the lead? As soon as the entities appeared they would have many many rifles aimed at them, they wouldn't last a second.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see how this can be a -J. It isn't funny, nor does it want to be. It's based on a meme, but it isn't actually humorous. More gorey if anything, considering the effects on videos with too many subjects in them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So grotesque! (In Japanese: すげえグロい！)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what did it for me. Nice vague description of the gore aspect of this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just want to know what happens in the actual W.A.N.O video. Robbie's lair is pretty big…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You win.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Im just glad Stephan Karl is now immortalized even in SCP format

;_;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This successfully creeped me out. More intellectually so than physically, but I think that's me.
It is a horrifying idea, being crushed like that. Much like drowning; it'd take a while and

The logs were weird(I always read them all), and up until the victims started running out of space, it was fairly believable, and then the gore started. It was not described in great detail, but it was effective.

The last bit, right at the end… That was the horror equivalent of a joke's punchline. That makes me think "Has this happened before? It looks like the two survivors are not the originals, so are these new fellers pure clones, or are they somehow malevolent?… Oh, no; if this has happened before, and they're malevolent, what're they planning?" and THAT's terrifying.

Have a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess you could say this SCP has some… memetic effects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a meme that's literally a memetic hazard. I freaking love this.

But as hilarious as it is, I think this should be a -J page. I can't think of an SCP that specifically references real-life examples of modern media, then incorporates it into a legitimate SCP. IMO, the SCP Universe shouldn't be that closely related to our own.

Upvoted anyways. This is going to be a favorite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beautiful

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Deleted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the more disturbing things I've read on here in a while. +1
What has god wrought on ye faire wiki

item.title: Meemz.
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for memes. Downvoted and then upvoted again for good article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seriously? I thought everyone realized those sucked like 5 years ago

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Colin Furze
just for fun

Title: So What IF loads of fireworks exploded in my Van

Video Description: A full-sized van is filled with fireworks, which are then lit to destroy the vehicle.

Notes: During the second playback, human figures outside the van doubled as usual. However, analysis showed that human hands were also visible against the windows of the vehicle as the fireworks were going off, with the pairs of hands doubling after each playback. Individuals outside the vehicle do not seem to notice.

item.title: Re: Review of this scip except every mention of containment is replaced with a method for killing 682
item.content:encoded:
This is memes.

https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=We+are+%231+except+1+is+replaced+with

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I'm still running across Rage Comics today. So, yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. In any case, who cares? More people should downvote this anyways.

item.title: Re: Review of this scip except every mention of containment is replaced with a method for killing 682
item.content:encoded: the what

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hell. That's what happens

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
People remember memes

That's debatable. Would you have remembered the Rage Comics had I not just mentioned it to make it point?

item.title: Review of this scip except every mention of containment is replaced with a method for killing 682
item.content:encoded:
So yeah. Basically reads a lot like 1733, but with a meme added. (Like I have room to talk after the scips I've written.) I actually had to look up what Alphabet, Inc is in order to understand the

Flood 682's

Rapidly alternate between intense heat and cold. I'm basically gambling that two machines each dedicated to a specific function can alter the nature of the attack faster than a single rapidly-healing organism can adapt to it.

cell with sulfuric acid, since that apparently never doesn't work, since it's part of the default

Create a drill bit, with a Tungsten-carbide tip, followed by a stainless steel haft coated in Sodium. Drive this directly into the beast. If its body

Drop a huge block of ice on it. While it's adapting its body to cope with cold and the crushing weight, electrocute it from below.

any water, it dies horribly. note that it won't catch on fire. It will be too busy exploding to catch on fire.

procedures. Then while 682 is focused on regeneration to counteract the acid, you lower what I can only describe as giant steel blender blades into the chamber and pulse that fucker. It can choose between acid resistance, regeneration, or slashing resistance, but it probably can't do all three at the same time.

procedures. I'm not really sure if it's possible to

Just nuke it. We have a Reset Button, so it's worth a try, isn't it?

a literally viral internet video in the digital information age. The internet is just too decentralized. You know, the Streisand Effect, and all that. Still, overall an enjoyable read. And not even half as OP as 3002, so it's not even the hardest to

Give Dr. King a flamethrower and send him in there to combat it. When 682 turns into the mother of all apple seeds, roast it.

example to come out of this contest!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if it is linked with a livestream?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, honestly if anything it feels like something AWCY would do, especially after reading roget's explanation up there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
People remember memes. I don't think that's going to be a problem.

More importantly, this doesn't rely on the meme to carry the narrative. It's based on a meme, yes, but the actual effect it's about isn't.
